title: Marquee, Bento, Flip Text, Gravity Cursor, Toast Stack
description: Five live UI demos: infinite marquee, bento hover-expand, 3D word cycler, gravity particles, and stacked toasts.

drops.title: Infinite Velocity Marquee
drops.description: Three rows of brand names scrolling at different speeds in alternating directions. Hover to pause, click to reverse.
drops.concept: A classic infinite horizontal marquee reimagined with three parallel rows running at different velocities and opposing directions. The seamless loop relies on doubling the DOM content so the CSS animation never shows a seam. Hover any row to pause; click it to reverse. Inspired by the GSAP+Lenis infinite scroll technique from Codrops May 2026.

drops.title: Bento Grid Hover-Expand
drops.description: CSS Grid bento layout where hovering any card pops it forward and dims the rest with smooth spring-like transitions.
drops.concept: A 7-card asymmetric CSS Grid layout in the 'Bento Box' style popularized by Apple and Vercel. On hover, CSS transform scale pops the focused card forward while siblings dim to 45% opacity and scale down slightly. No JavaScript needed for the core effect — pure CSS transitions with configurable easing via debug panel.

drops.title: Split-Flap Word Cycler
drops.description: Airport departure board–style flip animation cycling through curated phrases. Six independent displays with staggered timing.
drops.concept: Replicates the split-flap electromechanical display boards found in train stations and airports, using CSS 3D transforms and backface-visibility. Each slot independently flips between words on a configurable interval. The three-row sentence structure creates a generative-text feel where each combination produces a new message. Click any slot to manually advance it.

drops.title: Gravity Particle Cursor
drops.description: Moving the cursor spawns particle trails that arc, fall under configurable gravity, and bounce off the canvas floor.
drops.concept: A Canvas 2D particle system where cursor movement continuously emits colored particles with semi-random initial velocity. Each particle experiences configurable gravity and bounces off the floor with energy loss, creating a naturalistic arc-and-settle motion. Click anywhere for a burst explosion. A custom CSS cursor (mix-blend-mode:difference) keeps the interaction feeling seamless.

interaction.mouse: move to spawn trails, click for burst
interaction.touch: touch-move to spawn, tap to burst

drops.title: Sonner-Style Stacked Toast
drops.description: Glassmorphic notifications that stack in 3D depth — newest at front, older compress behind. Drag right to dismiss.
drops.concept: A faithful reimplementation of the 'Sonner' stacking toast pattern (popularized by Emil Kowalski / shadcn/ui) in plain vanilla JS/CSS. Each new notification slides in from below and pushes previous toasts backward with a scale+y-offset transform, creating convincing 3D depth. Progress bars track auto-dismiss timing. Pointer drag with velocity threshold handles swipe-to-dismiss.

interaction.mouse: click type buttons to spawn; drag toast right to dismiss
